date_notes: Deal confirmed by CBC from anonymous sources; formal announcement pending late May 2026.
title_en: Alberta and federal government near deal on industrial carbon pricing at $130 per tonne by 2040
title_fr: L'Alberta et le gouvernement fédéral sur le point de s'entendre sur une tarification industrielle du carbone à 130 $/tonne d'ici 2040
description_en: Alberta and the federal government agree to an effective industrial carbon price of $130/tonne under Alberta's TIER system by 2040. The current effective price in Alberta is approximately $45/tonne, below the headline price of $95/tonne, attributed by critics to an oversupply of credits. The Canadian Climate Institute states the 2040 timeline is insufficient for Canada's net-zero targets.

relations.note: Negotiated resolution after years of Smith-federal confrontation on carbon pricing.

verification_notes: Update when formally announced; remove outcome-pending flag when signed.

claim_contested_by.counter_claim: Canadian Climate Institute states $130/tonne by 2040 is insufficient for Canada to reach net-zero by 2050.
